channel.title: Business - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.description:

  2. How much AI-driven productivity growth do we want?  The Edge Singapore
  3. Torsten Slok Says AI Lacks Productivity Gains  Let's Data Science
  4. AI-led investment supports US growth outlook to 2030: Deloitte  Fibre2Fashion
  5. Frontier Radar #2: Why AI productivity gets lost between benchmarks and the balance sheet  The Decoder

item.title: Oracle 'Survivors' Reportedly Told to 'Stretch' After Massive Layoffs—But Workers Are Refusing Extra Hours - International Business Times UK
item.description:
1. Oracle 'Survivors' Reportedly Told to 'Stretch' After Massive Layoffs—But Workers Are Refusing Extra Hours  International Business Times UK
  2. Read the Email Oracle Is Sending to Laid-Off Employees  Business Insider
  3. Oracle cutting thousands in latest layoff round as company continues to ramp AI spending  CNBC
  4. Outrage as Oracle files thousands of foreign-worker requests amid layoff bloodbath  New York Post
  5. Oracle layoffs: Reason the 6AM email from “Oracle leadership” told thousands of laid-off employees; 'After careful consideration of…  timesofindia.indiatimes.com

item.title: Iran war exposes frailties of 'no-hire' US economy - Reuters
item.description:
1. Iran war exposes frailties of 'no-hire' US economy  Reuters
  2. The US Labor Market Is Still in a Vulnerable Place  Bloomberg.com
  3. US employers added a surprisingly strong 178,000 jobs last month, rebounding from a weak February  AP News
  4. America’s frozen jobs market could stay on ice due to Iran war | CNN Business  CNN
  5. Will the Iran war slow U.S. hiring? Economists say risks are rising.  CBS News
